entries.notes: Per-object lifecycle attached to a scene node. Mounts once, ticks per frame while play mode is on, tears down when the node unmounts.
deviations: no inheritance — scripts are factory registrations, not subclasses
lifecycle is `onMount` / `onTick` / `onUnmount`, not Unity's Awake/Start/Update/OnDestroy quadruple

entries.notes: Adapter contract (Rapier / Cannon-es first-class). <RigidBody> binds a scene node to a physics body; ctx.physics gives the script raycast + query access.
deviations: adapter-pluggable — Unity is single-engine; vibesmith ships Rapier + Cannon-es and validates the contract against both
no built-in continuous collision detection toggle (Rapier's default is sufficient for the framework's tier-0 budgets)

entries.notes: DOM overlay rendered above the R3F canvas. Pure React + Tailwind; absolute-positioned, no 3D z-fighting concerns.
deviations: DOM-rendered, not engine-rendered — uses CSS layout instead of the engine's UI system
no separate 'world-space UI' mode (use a billboard <Sprite> for that)

entries.originalReason: Adapter abstraction is intentional — Unity / Godot couple networking to a specific transport (NetCode for GameObjects / High-Level Multiplayer API). vibesmith's adapter contract lets consumers swap Colyseus / WebSockets / WebRTC without rewriting the game.
entries.notes: Pluggable contract: state-delta replication + intent dispatch + reconciliation envelope. Consumer picks the transport.
deviations: transport-agnostic — Colyseus / raw WS / WebRTC all implement the same contract
the framework owns wire-version negotiation + the intent / state-delta / reconciliation shape, not the transport

entries.originalReason: AI-orchestrated content authoring is novel — neither Unity / Godot / Unreal nor Bevy ship a primitive for 'AI agent proposes content interventions; framework writes them to disk with provenance.' Closest analogue is Unity's PrefabUtility-applies-prefab-overrides, but that's user-driven, not agent-driven.
entries.notes: AI-assisted scene-graph intervention surface. Recipe → director-task-io substrate → human-gated apply. Backbone of the framework's AI-maximalist methodology.
deviations: no analogue in existing engines — entirely framework-original
provenance-first: every intervention records source/model/prompt/seed before applying

entries.notes: Content unit — recipe + generator + director + AI brief + preview. Per `docs/prefab-system.md`.
deviations: Unity-style 'prefab' name, but the shape is recipe+generator pairs not a frozen instance — closer to Godot's PackedScene + a generator script
ships with an AI brief + preview slot for content directors

entries.notes: Scene-tree of nodes with transform + script attachments + child references. Persisted as JSON; loaded by the binary at project open.
deviations: JSON-on-disk, not engine-binary — every scene is human-readable + git-diffable
no nested-prefab variants — overrides happen at the script-props layer (see scriptProps in slice 2 of UX-5)

entries.originalReason: Snapshot-driven dev — launch-into-state + HMR-preserved iteration — has no direct engine analogue. Closest is Unity's PlayMode + custom save-game systems, but those aren't first-class authoring substrate.
entries.notes: First-class capture + replay of game state. Unifies dev iteration + probe inputs + bug repros.
deviations: framework-managed format — consumer-shaped state shapes register via registerWorldModelStore
HMR-preserved: editing a script doesn't drop the snapshot's runtime state

entries.notes: Cast a ray from origin + direction; returns the first hit + its body handle. Standard collision query across every engine.
Scene-tree components for particle effects. CPU instanced-billboards (LOW/MEDIUM tier) + WebGPU-compute (HIGH/ULTRA tier) behind one consumer surface.
deviations: two backends behind one consumer API — CPU fallback automatic on WebGL 2
recipe-canon-driven: useVfxRecipe pulls curated effect presets

entries.notes: State machine + blend tree over Three's AnimationMixer. ctx.animator(id) gives scripts parameter-binding access.
deviations: JSON-as-data graphs (TS-as-data factory) instead of Unity's binary .controller assets
no Animator override controllers — variants live in the parent graph

entries.notes: Scene-tree audio source with 3D positional panning. Fixed mixer bus hierarchy (master / music / sfx / dialogue / ambient).
deviations: Web Audio under the hood — autoplay-gesture deferral built in
fixed bus hierarchy (no consumer-defined buses in v1) — keeps the mixer panel surface simple
